differential_diagnoses: Leptomeningeal relapse of diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL)
next_steps: Perform PCR testing of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) for JC virus, VZV, and a comprehensive viral panel (including arboviruses)
Perform CSF cytology and flow cytometry for repeat occult malignancy assessment
Send CSF for anti-neuronal antibody panel (paraneoplastic/autoimmune evaluation)
Consider brain biopsy of the affected cerebellar/brainstem region if non-invasive testing remains unrevealing
Continue supportive care and monitor neurological status closely, as the patient is immunocompromised

differential_diagnoses: Leptomeningeal relapse of DLBCL (potentially false-negative flow cytometry)
next_steps: Perform CSF metagenomic next-generation sequencing (mNGS) for pathogen identification
Order CSF JC virus PCR and a comprehensive viral panel
Repeat CSF cytology and flow cytometry with larger volume collection to improve diagnostic yield for lymphoma
Measure CSF IgG index and test for oligoclonal bands
Conduct thin-slice contrast-enhanced MRI of the brain and spine to better evaluate for subtle leptomeningeal enhancement
Consider a brain biopsy of the affected cerebellar cortex if non-invasive investigations remain inconclusive
Maintain empiric treatment coverage for VZV and potential opportunistic infections until results are available
